submissions.assignment_name: Moving from CodePen to VS Code (Jupiter Moons)

submission_comments.comment:
Good start, Jay!
You need to create an index.html file with the HTML in it and link it to the CSS file.
Let me know if you need any support!

You've made awesome progress, Jay!
You still need to change the src for all of the images from the link to a file path. 
Let me know if you need support on this!

Hi Jay,
Your index.html file is missing the boilerplate code (the structure that appears when you type html:5 and press tab). There are also some bugs in the HTML that's currently there, and the images are still using URLs instead of file paths.
Let's work through this together during office hours, lab time, or Focus Friday so we can get everything set up correctly. I'm happy to walk through it with you!

submissions.assignment_name: Moving from CodePen to VS Code (Jupiter Moons)

submission_comments.comment:
Good work, Anais!
Just one thing: you linked to css/style.css instead of css/styles.css. This is a great example of why we recommend either copying the relative path or using the method I showed you—it helps avoid small typos like this!
Once you link it correctly, I'll mark this as complete. Let me know if you need any support with this.

Great job, Anais! Now your files are correctly linked.
However, it looks like everything was deleted from the <body> in index.html, and all of the images were deleted from the images folder. Please add those back and resubmit.
Let me know if you need any support!
Awesome work!!

submissions.assignment_name: Moving from CodePen to VS Code (Jupiter Moons)

submission_comments.comment:
Hi ZeeZee,
You submitted a zip file of only index.html. Please zip the entire project folder for the jupiter moons project and submit that.
Thanks!

Hi ZeeZee,
This is better! A few things to clean up:

You have an index 2.html file—this can be deleted
You have an index.html zip file inside this folder—that can be deleted too
You don't have an images folder within this folder, so the paths to the images aren't working
You called this folder "portfolio"—it might make more sense to name it something like "jupiter moons"

Let me know if you need any support!

submissions.assignment_name: Morning Warmup: CodePen to VS Code (Artists)

submission_comments.comment:
Great start, Lucia!
Your images folder is inside of your css folder. Please move it so it's on the same level as the css folder.
Your images are all saved in the images folder, which is great! However, you need to change all of the image src attributes to the relative file path rather than the URL that's currently there.
Let me know if you need any support in making these changes.
Good job!!

submissions.assignment_name: Morning Warmup: CodePen to VS Code (Artists)

submission_comments.comment:
Great start, Dee!
You currently have a file called css rather than a folder called css with a styles.css file within it. Once you have that in place, you need to link the HTML to the CSS.
You can refer to this document: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1dt38XXyykZcKqfv1DDsuoKzRmzIp755uKXPxLN2H79M/edit?usp=sharing
Let me know if you need any support.

Hi Dee,
Great job linking the CSS! However, it looks like you have the HTML boilerplate code (<!DOCTYPE html>, <html>, <head>, <body>, etc.) repeated multiple times throughout your file. You should only have one set of these tags at the very top of your document.
Please remove the duplicate boilerplate code and keep only the first set at the top.
Let me know if you need help cleaning this up during lab time!
the extra plates should be gone now

okay i checked it, this should be it!
...if not, i need i support

Awesome Dee! You got it! 
Your warmup-3 folder has a whole warmup.zip file inside of it -- you can remove that as you shouldn't have a project inside of a project.

Push your changes to GitHub after you make these updates

submission_comments.comment:
Great work Tali! I love that you went above and beyond by adding other content.
Please fix the error in the HTML and format your HTML and CSS and I can mark this as complete.
Quick note on the links you added: 'Visit Freebird Farms' and 'Visit Three Duck Ranch' shouldn't be wrapped in a <nav> element since they're external links to other websites. The <nav> element is specifically for navigation within your own site. Great instinct to add those links though!
Great job!
Great work!
Great start! Please fix the errors in the HTML and the CSS.
Great work Shannon!
Great work!
Great work!

Great start, Jay!
A few things to add:

Set up CSS variables and use the provided colors
Add links to your nav list items
You're using a class of .nav-links in your CSS, but it's not in your HTML—make sure to add it or update your CSS to match what's in your HTML

Let me know if you need any support with these updates!

Hi Jay,
It looks like the items I mentioned in my previous comments still need to be addressed.
Let's work through these together if you need support—I'm available during lab times, office hours, or Focus Friday to go over them with you!

Hi Jay,
I noticed the same things I mentioned in the previous two comments. Perhaps you made the changes and forgot to save the CodePen. Let's discuss today in office hours :).

Good work!

Assignment

#2: bonus

submission_comments.comment: Awesome job!!!

Great start Tali!!
Reminder of the things we talked about in the breakout room:

Identify the content for each cell. So specify if it is a heading, image, link, etc.
Some of the grid children are  also grid parents (a grid inside a grid). Please mark those up accordingly.

If you need any support let me know :)
Great work!

This looks great!
One of the grid children is also a grid parents and has their own grid children (in the home depot site).
Please reflect that in your annotations and then I can mark this as complete!
Let me know if you need any support :)
Beautiful!

Great start!!
Make sure to include all of the information that the instructions ask for:

# of columns
# of rows
All of the content inside each cell. In your first link, you annotated the img but nothing else in the cell 

In your second link, please mark up the grids inside of the grid as well

Great work! 
There is just one thing left to do:
In the amazon halloween link, column 4 row 1 is a grid child but it's also a grid parent with its own columns, rows, and cards within it.  Please mark that up accordingly. 
Let me know if you need any support :)

Great work!
Side note: The second column isn't a grid, only the 1st, 3rd, and 4th are grids.To make things clear, I would put the column numbers 1-4 up top and then within the smaller grids add the column numbers for that grid. I'm not going to have you redo/resubmit it, but I want you to understand what I mean. 
I'm going to go over this in class as well

This is a great start!
There are just a couple of things missing:

Please write the column numbers and row numbers across the top and side
Annotate the grid children (you currently only have the grid parents annotated)

Let me know if you need any support :)
Great work!
Great work!
Great work!

Will you please upload the link(s) to your canva instead of the png?
Thank you!
Good work!
I think I did this right - hoping 💜

Great work!!
There are just a few notes:

For the 800px media query, you only need to have 3 columns instead of 4. 
Format the CSS
You set a gap on the wrapper on line 14, so you don't need to set a gap again on line 20 and 66. 

Let me know if you need any support.
Great work!
Awesome work!
Great work!

Great start, Lucia!
You have some errors in your CSS that are preventing one of your media queries from working.
Also, it looks like you may have accidentally removed the original style on the div with a class of .h, so it isn't showing up on the screen.
Please format your code before resubmitting.
Let me know if you need any support!
Great work!
Hi Jay, great job on the mobile and tablet layouts. Your desktop does not match the design. I'm happy to bug hunt together during lab if you want. :)

Great work! I see that you've been learning some CSS Grid syntax on your own too which is awesome!! 
A couple of tips for future projects:

You can set up the grid with one column and the gap for the mobile version outside of the media query
You can also make only the cards a grid and not include the h1 and p in it

Keep up the great work exploring new CSS techniques!

You can set up the grid with one column and the gap for the mobile version outside of the media query. Then inside the media query, just change the columns to have 3 instead of 1.

Keep up the great work!

Great work Anais! Please format your HTML and CSS and then I can mark this as complete.
A tip for the future:
Because of the cascade, you don't need to declare the same styles in a media query that you already have outside of it. 
For example, you already made .cards display as a grid and have a gap of 1rem. So you don't need to also write that in the media query.
Great job!!

This is a great start!!!  There are just a few things that need to be fixed: The
tag should be surrounding the and the
To center the wrapper, look at the link in the instructions and do it that way. Using align-items: center as you do on line 17 only works with something called flexbox which we will learn next week.  To make a gap between the button and the h1, you need to use a different property -- the gap property only works with CSS grid. Hi Jay,
Please carefully re-read the project instructions and compare your work to the reference image we provided. If your project doesn't look like the reference image, that's a sign there are requirements missing.

Great job Anais!
There are two instructions that are either missing or need fixing:

Part 2, instruction 4i
Part 2, instruction 7. You have created a gap using padding but it should be margin. They do both work to give space between the two elements - but padding adds space inside the element whereas margin makes space between the elements. 

Let me know if you have any questions or need support on this. Thanks!
 
3rem gap should be margin not padding
not sure if something is off since the button looks bigger than how the sample shows it.

Looks great!
It did that because all of the links are now displayed as block so they are taking up the full width of their container (which is the div with the class of wrapper). If you don't like that, you could put a class on all the links except for the button and only make those display as blocks. 
But either way this is complete!

Great start!
There are several things:

You have hubble as the button instead of contact. It looks like you may have lost one of the list items. There should be home, spitzer, james webb, and hubble and then contact which would be styled as a button
You should not use the <button> html tag to style this. Keep it as a link with the <a> tag and use a class of button, since when we click the "button" it just takes us to another page rather than running any functionality.
You imported the Barlow font but didn't use it in the CSS
To put space around each link, you may want to apply the padding to li rather than the nav a. We can talk about why in office hours (it is an inline and block thing)
Look at instruction 4 c i again
The main element should include the h1, as it's also part of the main part of the page
You added a gap around the entire heading instead of just between the button and heading

I know this is a lot of information, so please let me know if you have questions or need support. We can also go over all of this together in office hours tomorrow.

Hi Dee, for the google fonts, please use the @import statement in your CSS file, rather than a link in your HTML. You can refer to the codepens we did together in class for the method we use.Mostly, this is important in Codepen because those link tags you put in your HTML belong nested inside the <head> tag, which we don't have access to in Codepen. submission_comments.comment: Hi Tali, this seems to be the wrong link. Please resubmit with the link to the correct codepen.
Great work!

Great work Dara!
List items are block elements -- you can tell by putting a border around it and seeing how much space it takes up.
The way you centered the content using 
margin: 0 auto; 
is a great way to do it! It's awesome that you did your own research and found that in the MDN.

This is awesome Shannon!
You do have an error in your HTML so please analyze HTML in codepen, fix it, and resubmit.
It's awesome that you found new tags to italicize the members of the genus Prunus list. However, we are asking you to italicize using CSS, not HTML. See if you can use a CSS property to italicize them instead of using the <i> tag. Down the line or at office hours, we can talk about why it is "best practice" to only use html elements for purposes of structure and not style.
Great work!

Great start Lucia!
There are a few things:

There is a few errors in the CSS. After you fix one, remember to re-analyze the css as other errors may be detected after you fix one.
Remember to format HTML
You don't need to wrap a div around the list items since you already have a ul around them that groups them
Re-read the instructions as you are missing about 4 of them -- some of them may not be working due to the errors in your CSS.

Let me know if you need any support!
Great work!

Great start, Dee! This is looking great.
Two things:

In the javascript box, it asks you to tell us if certain elements are block or inline. Please write the answer to that in that box next to each item.
You may need to add another element to the HTML to do instruction #8. The entire pink box should be 500px and the body behind it should still be white.

Let me know if you need any support :)
Great work!
Hopefully it is done right

Great work Jay!
For step 2 where you listed whether elements were inline or block -- the correct answer is that they are all block elements except for <dfn>, which is an inline element. To be able to tell, put a border around the elements (temporarily) so you can see how much space they take up by default.

Great work Anais! I see you are also learning flexbox already and playing with CSS on your own time which is AWESOME!
There is one issue: on line 17, you used 
text-indent: -2rem;
there is still an indent, but now it is in a different direction. 
You need to use the margin css property to get rid of the indent.
Once you do that and resubmit, this will be marked as complete.
 
I also want to clarify a few CSS properties/values.
On line 12, you have
font-weight: bold;
which makes text bold. Then on line 13 you have
font-style:bold;
which is not doing anything. font-style can be used to set text to italic. you can read about it here.
 
Let me know if you need any support :)
Great job!
Hi Zeezee, your resubmission is the wrong link.
Great work, your blog post is really thoughtful!
Great job ZeeZee! I'm so glad you are studying in your free time and writing your understanding of the concepts in your own words -- that's an amazing way to learn!
I am SO sorry I thought I submitted this in 🙏
Great job!
